info.name: CYO Adventure API
info.description: Sequential API test suite for CYO Adventure, run against a freshly migrated and seeded dev database (scripts/seed_dev_data.py). Folders run in an order that tells one coherent scenario; run with --bail so the first failing assertion stops the suite.

exec: pm.test("database check passed", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.status).to.eql("ok"); pm.expect(body.checks.database.status).to.eql(true); });
pm.test("error body names AuthenticationError", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.error).to.eql("AuthenticationError"); });
pm.test("error body names AuthenticationError", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.error).to.eql("AuthenticationError"); });
pm.test("error body names AuthorizationError", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.error).to.eql("AuthorizationError"); });
pm.test("identity matches dev-guardian", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.subject).to.eql("dev-guardian"); pm.expect(body.role).to.eql("guardian"); });
pm.test("identity matches dev-child", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.subject).to.eql("dev-child"); pm.expect(body.role).to.eql("child"); });
pm.test("identity matches dev-admin", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.subject).to.eql("dev-admin"); pm.expect(body.role).to.eql("admin"); });

pm.test("dev profile is present", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.profiles.length).to.be.above(0); var dev = body.profiles.find(function (p) { return p.display_name === "Dev Reader"; }); pm.expect(dev).to.not.be.undefined; pm.expect(dev.age_band).to.eql("10-13"); pm.collectionVariables.set("profile_id", dev.id); });

pm.test("echoes the created profile", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.display_name).to.eql("Newman Sibling"); pm.expect(body.age_band).to.eql("5-8"); pm.collectionVariables.set("new_profile_id", body.id); });
pm.test("display_name updated", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.display_name).to.eql("Newman Sibling Updated"); });

item.name: GET /profiles as child (scoped to own profile only)

exec: pm.test("child sees exactly its own profile", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.profiles.length).to.eql(1); pm.expect(body.profiles[0].id).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get("profile_id")); });
pm.test("error body names AuthorizationError", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.error).to.eql("AuthorizationError"); });

item.name: child-sessions
item.description: Guardian-minted child session tokens (P6-04): mint one for the seeded profile, prove the token acts as that child, then pin the role, cross-family, and missing-profile gates.

item.name: POST child-sessions mints a session for the dev profile

exec: pm.test("token is scoped to the requested profile", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.profile_id).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get("profile_id")); pm.expect(body.token).to.be.a("string"); pm.expect(body.token.length).to.be.above(20); pm.collectionVariables.set("child_session_token", body.token); });

item.name: GET /me with the minted token acts as the child

exec: pm.test("principal is the child, scoped to one profile", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.role).to.eql("child"); pm.expect(body.is_admin).to.eql(false); pm.expect(body.profile_ids.length).to.eql(1); pm.expect(body.profile_ids[0]).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get("profile_id")); pm.expect(body.family_id).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get("family_id")); });
pm.test("error body names AuthorizationError", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.error).to.eql("AuthorizationError"); });

item.name: POST child-sessions for another family's profile -> 403

exec: pm.test("error body names AuthorizationError", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.error).to.eql("AuthorizationError"); });

item.name: POST child-sessions as admin for a nonexistent profile -> 404

exec: pm.test("error body names ResourceNotFoundError", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.error).to.eql("ResourceNotFoundError"); });

item.name: device-grants
item.description: Durable device authorizations (ADR-014 phase 1): mint a grant, walk the device token's exact capability matrix (list profiles and mint a child session, nothing else), then revoke and prove revocation is enforced online.

item.name: POST device-grants as guardian mints a grant

exec: pm.test("grant is bound to the guardian's family", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.family_id).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get("family_id")); pm.expect(body.token.length).to.be.above(20); pm.collectionVariables.set("device_grant_token", body.token); pm.collectionVariables.set("device_grant_id", body.id); });

item.name: GET device-grants lists the grant without its token

exec: pm.test("row is present and never carries the token", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); var found = body.find(function (g) { return g.id === pm.collectionVariables.get("device_grant_id"); }); pm.expect(found).to.not.be.undefined; pm.expect(found.label).to.eql("Newman Test Tablet"); pm.expect(found).to.not.have.property("token"); });

item.name: GET /profiles with the device token lists family profiles

exec: pm.test("device sees the family profile picker", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); var ids = body.profiles.map(function (p) { return p.id; }); pm.expect(ids).to.include(pm.collectionVariables.get("profile_id")); });

item.name: POST child-sessions with the device token mints a session

exec: pm.test("device-minted session targets the profile", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.profile_id).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get("profile_id")); });

item.name: POST device-grants with the device token (wrong role) -> 403

exec: pm.test("error body names AuthorizationError", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.error).to.eql("AuthorizationError"); });

item.name: POST onboarding with the device token -> 403

exec: pm.test("error body names AuthorizationError", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.error).to.eql("AuthorizationError"); });

item.name: POST device-grants as admin without family_id -> 422

exec: pm.test("error body names ValidationError", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.error).to.eql("ValidationError"); });
pm.test("no body on revocation", function () { pm.expect(pm.response.text()).to.eql(""); });

item.name: GET /profiles with the revoked device token -> 401

exec: pm.test("error body names AuthenticationError", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.error).to.eql("AuthenticationError"); });

item.name: DELETE device-grants for an unknown grant -> 404

exec: pm.test("error body names ResourceNotFoundError", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.error).to.eql("ResourceNotFoundError"); });

item.name: onboarding
item.description: First-login guardian provisioning (P6-03): a fresh verified subject gets a family + guardian row (201), a retry is idempotent (200), and child-session tokens can never onboard.

item.name: POST onboarding provisions a new guardian family

exec: pm.test("a fresh family and guardian are created", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.created).to.eql(true); pm.expect(body.role).to.eql("guardian"); pm.collectionVariables.set("onboard_family_id", body.family_id); pm.collectionVariables.set("onboard_user_id", body.user_id); });

pm.test("the same family and user come back, not a duplicate", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.created).to.eql(false); pm.expect(body.family_id).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get("onboard_family_id")); pm.expect(body.user_id).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get("onboard_user_id")); });

item.name: POST onboarding with a child session token -> 403

exec: pm.test("error body names AuthorizationError", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.error).to.eql("AuthorizationError"); });
pm.test("error body names AuthenticationError", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.error).to.eql("AuthenticationError"); });

pm.test("families are present and name-ordered", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.families.length).to.be.above(0); pm.expect(body.families[0].name).to.eql("Dev Family"); });
pm.test("error body names AuthorizationError", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.error).to.eql("AuthorizationError"); });

pm.test("new family starts active and empty", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.name).to.eql("Newman Ops Family"); pm.expect(body.status).to.eql("active"); pm.expect(body.guardian_count).to.eql(0); pm.expect(body.kid_count).to.eql(0); pm.collectionVariables.set("newman_family_id", body.id); });

pm.test("rename is applied without touching status", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.name).to.eql("Newman Ops Family Renamed"); pm.expect(body.status).to.eql("active"); });
pm.test("error body names AuthorizationError", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.error).to.eql("AuthorizationError"); });

item.name: PATCH admin/families for an unknown family -> 404

exec: pm.test("error body names ResourceNotFoundError", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.error).to.eql("ResourceNotFoundError"); });

item.name: GET /library as guardian (four seeded published stories)

exec: pm.test("all seeded published stories are present", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); var tide = body.stories.find(function (s) { return s.title === "The Tide Pool Mystery"; }); var clockwork = body.stories.find(function (s) { return s.title === "The Clockwork Garden"; }); var ember1 = body.stories.find(function (s) { return s.title === "Ember Trail 1"; }); var ember2 = body.stories.find(function (s) { return s.title === "Ember Trail 2"; }); pm.expect(tide).to.not.be.undefined; pm.expect(clockwork).to.not.be.undefined; pm.expect(ember1).to.not.be.undefined; pm.expect(ember2).to.not.be.undefined; pm.expect(body.stories.length).to.eql(4); pm.collectionVariables.set("storybook_id_1", tide.id); pm.collectionVariables.set("storybook_title_1", tide.title); pm.collectionVariables.set("storybook_id_2", clockwork.id); });
pm.test("response matches the Storybook blob schema", function () { pm.response.to.have.jsonSchema(schema); });

pm.test("blob id and title match the discovered library entry", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.id).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get("storybook_id_1")); pm.expect(body.title).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get("storybook_title_1")); });
pm.test("start node exists in the node list", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); var ids = body.nodes.map(function (n) { return n.id; }); pm.expect(ids).to.include(body.start_node); });
pm.test("error body names AuthenticationError", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.error).to.eql("AuthenticationError"); });
pm.test("error body names ResourceNotFoundError", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.error).to.eql("ResourceNotFoundError"); });

item.name: PUT reading-state creates the first save

exec: pm.test("server-owned revision counter incremented", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.state_revision).to.eql(1); pm.expect(body.current_node).to.eql("n_start"); });
pm.test("saved state is returned", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.state_revision).to.eql(1); });
pm.test("ending recorded matches request", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.ending_id).to.eql("e_treasure"); });
pm.test("error body names AuthenticationError", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.error).to.eql("AuthenticationError"); });

item.name: GET /library discovers the Ember Trail series pair

exec: pm.test("both Ember Trail books are discoverable", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); var embers = body.stories.filter(function (s) { return s.series_id !== null && s.series_id !== undefined; }).sort(function (a, b) { return a.book_index - b.book_index; }); pm.expect(embers.length).to.eql(2); pm.collectionVariables.set("ember_book_1", embers[0].id); pm.collectionVariables.set("ember_book_2", embers[1].id); });

item.name: GET series-next from Ember Trail 1 points at book 2

exec: pm.test("book 1 continues into book 2 with carried state", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.next).to.not.eql(null); pm.expect(body.next.storybook_id).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get("ember_book_2")); pm.expect(body.next.version).to.eql(1); pm.expect(body.next.carries_state).to.eql(true); });

item.name: GET series-next from the final series book is null

exec: pm.test("no next book after the series finale", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.next).to.eql(null); });

item.name: GET series-next on a non-series book is null

exec: pm.test("a standalone book has no continuation", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.next).to.eql(null); });

item.name: GET series-next for another family's profile -> 403

exec: pm.test("error body names AuthorizationError", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.error).to.eql("AuthorizationError"); });

item.name: GET series-next for an unknown storybook -> 404

exec: pm.test("error body names ResourceNotFoundError", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.error).to.eql("ResourceNotFoundError"); });
pm.test("rating value echoed", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.value).to.eql(5); });
pm.test("at least one rating is present", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.ratings.length).to.be.above(0); });
pm.test("error body names AuthenticationError", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.error).to.eql("AuthenticationError"); });

item.name: GET assignments for storybook 1 (already assigned by seed)

exec: pm.test("dev profile is already assigned", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.profile_ids).to.include(pm.collectionVariables.get("profile_id")); });

item.name: GET guardian/books lists all published books

exec: pm.test("all four published books are listed", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.books.length).to.eql(4); });

item.name: POST assignments grants storybook 1 to the sibling profile

exec: pm.test("both profiles now assigned", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.profile_ids).to.include(pm.collectionVariables.get("profile_id")); pm.expect(body.profile_ids).to.include(pm.collectionVariables.get("new_profile_id")); });
pm.test("error body names AuthorizationError", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.error).to.eql("AuthorizationError"); });

item.name: POST story-requests submits a kid request

exec: pm.test("request is pending", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.status).to.eql("pending"); pm.collectionVariables.set("request_id", body.id); });

item.name: GET story-requests?status=pending includes the new request

exec: pm.test("the new pending request is listed", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); var mine = body.requests.find(function (r) { return r.id === pm.collectionVariables.get("request_id"); }); pm.expect(mine).to.not.be.undefined; });
pm.test("status is declined", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.status).to.eql("declined"); });

item.name: POST story-requests submits a second kid request for approval

exec: pm.test("second request is pending", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.status).to.eql("pending"); pm.collectionVariables.set("request_id_2", body.id); });
pm.test("error body names AuthorizationError", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.error).to.eql("AuthorizationError"); });

item.name: POST story-requests/{id}/approve approves the second request

exec: pm.test("request approved with a linked concept", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.status).to.eql("approved"); pm.expect(body.concept_id).to.be.a("string").and.not.empty; });

item.name: POST story-requests/authored creates a pre-approved request

exec: pm.test("authored request is approved with a concept", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.status).to.eql("approved"); pm.expect(body.concept_id).to.be.a("string").and.not.empty; });
pm.test("error body names AuthorizationError", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.error).to.eql("AuthorizationError"); });

item.name: POST authoring-plan as admin creates an awaiting_manual_fill job

exec: pm.test("skill-mechanism job is parked for manual fill with a matched skeleton", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.status).to.eql("awaiting_manual_fill"); pm.expect(body.method).to.eql("skeleton_fill"); pm.expect(body.mechanism).to.eql("skill"); pm.expect(body.skeleton_slug).to.be.a("string").and.not.empty; pm.expect(body.request_id).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get("request_id_2")); });
pm.test("error body names StateTransitionError", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.error).to.eql("StateTransitionError"); });

item.name: GET admin/story-requests lists requests across families

exec: pm.test("both earlier requests appear in the global queue", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); var ids = body.requests.map(function (r) { return r.id; }); pm.expect(ids).to.include(pm.collectionVariables.get("request_id")); pm.expect(ids).to.include(pm.collectionVariables.get("request_id_2")); });
pm.test("error body names AuthorizationError", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.error).to.eql("AuthorizationError"); });

item.name: GET admin/story-requests with a bogus status -> 422

exec: pm.test("error body names ValidationError", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.error).to.eql("ValidationError"); });

item.name: GET review-queue lists the in-review story

exec: pm.test("the seeded flagged story is in the queue", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.items.length).to.be.above(0); var item = body.items.find(function (i) { return i.status === "in_review"; }); pm.expect(item).to.not.be.undefined; pm.expect(item.flagged_count).to.be.above(0); pm.collectionVariables.set("storybook_id_review", item.storybook_id); });

item.name: GET review surface for the flagged story

exec: pm.test("flagged passage is present", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.status).to.eql("in_review"); pm.expect(body.flagged_passages.length).to.be.above(0); });

item.name: POST send-back returns the story for revision

exec: pm.test("status is needs_revision", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.status).to.eql("needs_revision"); });
pm.test("status is in_review again", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.status).to.eql("in_review"); });

pm.test("status is published with an approver", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.status).to.eql("published"); pm.expect(body.approved_by).to.be.a("string").and.not.empty; });
pm.test("error body names AuthorizationError", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.error).to.eql("AuthorizationError"); });
pm.test("job is queued", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.status).to.eql("queued"); pm.collectionVariables.set("job_id", body.job_id); });
pm.test("the family job list is non-empty", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.jobs.length).to.be.above(0); });
pm.test("job id matches", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.id).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get("job_id")); });

item.name: POST validate re-runs the gate on a published version

exec: pm.test("a published version is not blocked", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.blocked).to.eql(false); });
pm.test("error body names AuthorizationError", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.error).to.eql("AuthorizationError"); });
pm.test("known categories are listed", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.known_categories.length).to.be.above(0); });
pm.test("override echoed back", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.min_verdict).to.eql("flag"); pm.expect(body.min_score).to.eql(0.3); });

pm.test("override no longer listed", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); var row = body.rows.find(function (r) { return r.age_band === "10-13" && r.category === "newman-test-category"; }); pm.expect(row).to.be.undefined; });
pm.test("seeded default is 0.05", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.value).to.eql(0.05); });

item.name: PUT noise-floor as admin updates the value

exec: pm.test("value updated", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.value).to.eql(0.1); });
pm.test("error body names AuthorizationError", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.error).to.eql("AuthorizationError"); });

item.name: moderation-dashboard
item.description: Admin moderation evidence (WS-F), read after the thresholds folder so its threshold and noise-floor writes appear in recent_changes.

exec: pm.test("recent changes carry only threshold event types", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); body.recent_changes.forEach(function (c) { pm.expect(["threshold_changed", "noise_floor_changed"]).to.include(c.event_type); }); });

item.name: GET admin/moderation/suggestions returns proposals and gates

exec: pm.test("the suggestion gates are the documented constants", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.min_decided_versions).to.eql(5); pm.expect(body.min_override_rate).to.eql(0.8); pm.expect(body.suggestions).to.be.an("array"); });
pm.test("error body names AuthorizationError", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.error).to.eql("AuthorizationError"); });
pm.test("error body names AuthorizationError", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.error).to.eql("AuthorizationError"); });

pm.test("entry created enabled", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.provider).to.eql("anthropic"); pm.expect(body.enabled).to.eql(true); pm.collectionVariables.set("allowlist_entry_id", body.id); });
pm.test("entry disabled", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.enabled).to.eql(false); });

pm.test("entry no longer listed", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); var row = body.rows.find(function (r) { return r.id === pm.collectionVariables.get("allowlist_entry_id"); }); pm.expect(row).to.be.undefined; });
pm.test("error body names AuthorizationError", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.error).to.eql("AuthorizationError"); });

item.name: admin-users
item.description: Admin console user management (WS-J): list adult accounts, invite a guardian into the newman family, and pin the self-manage, duplicate-invite, and pending-transition guards.

exec: pm.test("adult accounts only, never the authn subject", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.users.length).to.be.above(0); body.users.forEach(function (u) { pm.expect(u).to.not.have.property("authn_subject"); pm.expect(u.role).to.not.eql("child"); }); var admin = body.users.find(function (u) { return u.role === "admin"; }); pm.expect(admin).to.not.be.undefined; pm.collectionVariables.set("admin_user_id", admin.id); });

item.name: POST admin/users invites a guardian into the newman family

exec: pm.test("invite lands pending in the right family", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.status).to.eql("pending"); pm.expect(body.email).to.eql("newman.invite@example.com"); pm.expect(body.family_id).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get("newman_family_id")); pm.collectionVariables.set("invited_user_id", body.id); });

item.name: POST admin/users with the same email again -> 409

exec: pm.test("error body names StateTransitionError", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.error).to.eql("StateTransitionError"); });

item.name: PATCH admin/users grants the invite the admin capability

exec: pm.test("capability set without changing the base role", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.is_admin).to.eql(true); pm.expect(body.role).to.eql("guardian"); });

item.name: PATCH admin/users cannot leave pending via status -> 422

exec: pm.test("error body names ValidationError", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.error).to.eql("ValidationError"); });

item.name: PATCH admin/users on your own account -> 403

exec: pm.test("error body names AuthorizationError", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.error).to.eql("AuthorizationError"); });

item.name: GET admin/users filtered to the newman family

exec: pm.test("only the invited guardian is in the family", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.users.length).to.eql(1); pm.expect(body.users[0].id).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get("invited_user_id")); });
pm.test("error body names AuthorizationError", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.error).to.eql("AuthorizationError"); });

item.name: PATCH admin/users for an unknown user -> 404

exec: pm.test("error body names ResourceNotFoundError", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.error).to.eql("ResourceNotFoundError"); });

item.name: admin-profiles
item.description: Admin console child-profile management (WS-J): cross-family listing, create in the newman family, PIN + deactivation lifecycle, and the deactivated-profile child-session gate.

item.name: GET admin/profiles lists profiles across families

exec: pm.test("listing crosses family boundaries", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.profiles.length).to.be.above(1); var cross = body.profiles.find(function (p) { return p.family_id !== pm.collectionVariables.get("family_id"); }); pm.expect(cross).to.not.be.undefined; });

item.name: POST admin/profiles creates a profile in the newman family

exec: pm.test("profile lands active and PIN-less in the family", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.family_id).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get("newman_family_id")); pm.expect(body.status).to.eql("active"); pm.expect(body.has_pin).to.eql(false); pm.collectionVariables.set("admin_profile_id", body.id); });

item.name: PATCH admin/profiles sets a PIN and deactivates

exec: pm.test("PIN recorded as a flag only; profile deactivated", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.has_pin).to.eql(true); pm.expect(body.status).to.eql("deactivated"); pm.expect(pm.response.text()).to.not.include("1234"); });

item.name: PATCH admin/profiles with a malformed PIN -> 422

exec: pm.test("the malformed PIN is never echoed back", function () { pm.expect(pm.response.text()).to.not.include("abc"); });

item.name: POST child-sessions for the deactivated profile -> 404

exec: pm.test("error body names ResourceNotFoundError", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.error).to.eql("ResourceNotFoundError"); });

item.name: GET admin/profiles filtered to the newman family

exec: pm.test("deactivated profile still listed for admins", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.profiles.length).to.eql(1); pm.expect(body.profiles[0].id).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get("admin_profile_id")); pm.expect(body.profiles[0].status).to.eql("deactivated"); });
pm.test("error body names AuthorizationError", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.error).to.eql("AuthorizationError"); });

item.name: PATCH admin/profiles for an unknown profile -> 404

exec: pm.test("error body names ResourceNotFoundError", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.error).to.eql("ResourceNotFoundError"); });

item.name: family-connections
item.description: Directional cross-family recommendation opt-ins (WS-J): connect the dev family to the newman family, pin the duplicate/self/unknown guards, then remove the row.

exec: pm.test("no connections are seeded", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.connections.length).to.eql(0); });

pm.test("directional row carries both family names", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.family_id).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get("family_id")); pm.expect(body.connected_family_id).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get("newman_family_id")); pm.expect(body.family_name).to.eql("Dev Family"); pm.expect(body.connected_family_name).to.eql("Newman Ops Family Renamed"); pm.collectionVariables.set("connection_id", body.id); });
pm.test("error body names StateTransitionError", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.error).to.eql("StateTransitionError"); });
pm.test("error body names ValidationError", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.error).to.eql("ValidationError"); });
pm.test("error body names ResourceNotFoundError", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.error).to.eql("ResourceNotFoundError"); });
pm.test("error body names AuthorizationError", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.error).to.eql("AuthorizationError"); });
pm.test("no body on removal", function () { pm.expect(pm.response.text()).to.eql(""); });
pm.test("error body names ResourceNotFoundError", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.error).to.eql("ResourceNotFoundError"); });
pm.test("error body names AuthenticationError", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.error).to.eql("AuthenticationError"); });
pm.test("error body names AuthorizationError", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.error).to.eql("AuthorizationError"); });

item.name: GET cover as admin (DB-only status read)

exec: pm.test("no cover has been generated yet", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.cover_status).to.eql("none"); pm.expect(body.cover_url).to.be.null; });

item.name: GET cover for a nonexistent version -> 404

exec: pm.test("error body names ResourceNotFoundError", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.error).to.eql("ResourceNotFoundError"); });
pm.test("error body names AuthenticationError", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.error).to.eql("AuthenticationError"); });
pm.test("error body names AuthorizationError", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.error).to.eql("AuthorizationError"); });

item.name: archive
item.description: Storybook archive lifecycle on the second seeded published story. Runs LAST: archiving removes the book from the library, so earlier folders (library, reading, assignments) must complete before it disappears.

exec: pm.test("error body names AuthorizationError", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.error).to.eql("AuthorizationError"); });

item.name: POST archive as admin archives the published story

exec: pm.test("archived id matches the target book", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.id).to.eql(pm.collectionVariables.get("storybook_id_2")); pm.expect(body.status).to.eql("archived"); });
pm.test("error body names StateTransitionError", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); pm.expect(body.error).to.eql("StateTransitionError"); });

item.name: GET /library as guardian excludes the archived story

exec: pm.test("archived story is gone; the other published story remains", function () { var body = pm.response.json(); var ids = body.stories.map(function (s) { return s.id; }); pm.expect(ids).to.not.include(pm.collectionVariables.get("storybook_id_2")); pm.expect(ids).to.include(pm.collectionVariables.get("storybook_id_1")); });
